steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment where the metals concentrations measurements are directly representative or can be corrected so as to be representative of the total emissions from the affected facility.
Select representative measurement points or paths for monitoring in locations that the CEMS will pass the RA test.
Relocate the CEMS if the cause of failure to meet the RA test is determined to be the measurement location and a satisfactory correction technique cannot be established.

Ensure the measurement location is at least eight equivalent diameters downstream of the nearest control device, point of pollutant generation, bend, or other point at which a change of pollutant concentration or flow disturbance may occur.
Ensure the measurement location is at least two equivalent diameters upstream from the effluent exhaust.
Ensure the measurement point is no less than 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall.
Ensure the measurement point is within or centrally located over the centroidal area of the stack or duct cross section.
Select traverse points to determine the representativeness of the measurement location according to 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1.
Ensure the CEMS data recorder response range includes zero and a high level value equal to the span value.
Ensure the CEMS has the capability of providing multiple outputs with different high level values or automatically changing the high level value as required.
Ensure that the measured value does not exceed 95 percent of the high level value.
Ensure the RA of the CEMS is no greater than 20 percent of the mean value of the RM test data or 10 percent of the applicable standard, whichever is greater.
Ensure the CEMS design allows determination of calibration drift at concentration levels commensurate with the applicable emission standard.
Ensure CEMS calibration does not drift or deviate from the reference value by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for each metal.
Perform calibration at a point equal to 80 to 120 percent of the applicable emission standard for each metal.
Ensure the CEMS design allows determination of zero drift for each metal.
Ensure the CEMS zero point for each metal does not drift by more than 5 percent of the emission standard.
Continuously sample the stack effluent as per the specified emission regulation.
Ensure the averaging time and procedure for reporting complies with applicable emission regulations.
Ensure the response time for the CEMS does not exceed 2 minutes to achieve 95 percent of the final stable value.
Consider obtaining a waiver from the response time requirement for instantaneous, continuous CEMS.
Install the CEMS and prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day for 7 consecutive days according to the procedure given in Section 6.
Conduct a RA test following the CD test period according to the procedure given in Section 7.
Verify the initial performance of the entire CEMS system by comparison to RM measurements.
Conduct RM measurements to yield results representative of emissions from the source.
Conduct diluent, moisture, and pollutant measurements simultaneously, if applicable.
Note the beginning and end of each RM test period in the CEMS data log.
Correlate the CEMS and RM test data as to time and duration.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements for each metal required at each level required.
Report all data, including rejected data pairs.
Summarize the results on a data sheet.
Calculate the mean of the RM values.
Calculate the arithmetic differences between the RM and CEMS output sets.
Calculate the mean of the differences.
Calculate the standard deviation of each data set and CEMS RA using the equations in Section 8.
Calculate the arithmetic mean of a data set as follows:
Calculate the arithmetic mean of the difference, d, of a data set, using Equation 3 and substituting d for x.
Calculate the standard deviation (SD) of a data set as follows:
Calculate the RA as follows:
Summarize in tabular form the results of the CD tests and the RA tests or alternate RA procedure as appropriate.

preconditions.text: If the RM traverse line interferes with or is interfered by the CEMS measurements, the line may be displaced up to 30 cm from the centroidal area.
Before determining the CD and ZD, ensure the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load.
Before conducting the CD test, ensure no unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place for a 24-hour period.
Before conducting the RA test, ensure the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load.

constraints.text: None of the CD’s or ZD’s may exceed the specification.
Conduct the CD test immediately before any adjustments are made to the CEMS zero and calibration settings.
